title: Assemblers and routine operatives n.e.c.
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of agricultural, forestry, aquacultural, and related workers. Under supervision, perform manual labor necessary to develop, maintain, or protect areas such as forests, forested areas, woodlands, wetlands, and rangelands through such activities as raising and transporting seedlings; combating insects, pests, and diseases harmful to plant life; and building structures to control water, erosion, and leaching of soil. Includes forester aides, seedling pullers, tree planters, and gatherers of nontimber forestry products such as pine straw. (Based on 38 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Select trees to be cut down, assessing factors such as site, terrain, and weather conditions before beginning work.
Evaluate log characteristics and determine grades, using established criteria, and identify logs of substandard or special grade for regrading.
Paint identification marks on logs to specify grades, or call out grades to log markers.
Measure log lengths and mark boles for bucking into logs, according to specifications, while inspecting logs for defects.
Drive to sawmills, wharfs, or skids to inspect logs or pulpwood, and communicate with coworkers by signals to direct log movement.
Load logs or wood onto trucks or trailers by hand or using loaders, and secure cables or chains to logs for transport.
Drive tractors to drag or transport logs from felling sites to log landing areas for processing and loading, and drive for building or repairing logging roads.
Evaluate trees for characteristics like twist or rot, and determine how to control the direction of a tree's fall to minimize damage.
Inspect or test installed units or assemblies for fit, alignment, and performance using measuring instruments or test equipment.
Perform fire protection or suppression duties, such as constructing fire breaks or disposing of brush, and fight forest fires under supervision.
Maintain tallies of trees examined and counted during tree marking or measuring efforts.
Select or cut trees according to markings, sizes, types, or grades.
Select tree seedlings, prepare the ground, or plant trees in reforestation areas using manual planting tools.
Operate machinery and heavy equipment, such as forklifts, and assist in the production of wiring assemblies.
Supervise assemblers, train employees on job procedures, and determine work assignments and procedures.
Perform quality checks on products and parts and complete production reports to communicate team production levels to management.
Read work orders and blueprints to ensure work is performed according to specifications.
Disassemble items to prepare for finishing and remove old finishes and damaged parts using various tools.
Select appropriate finishing ingredients, and apply them to surfaces, ensuring quality and specifications are met.
Construct wooden models, templates, or patterns for parts and production tools and verify dimensions using measuring devices.
Replace worn parts and adjust equipment components using hand tools.
Measure and set stops, rolls, brushes, and guides on automatic feeders and conveying equipment using micrometers, rules, and hand tools.
Clean surfaces of workpieces in preparation for coating using cleaning fluids, solvents, brushes, scrapers, or sandpaper.
Apply coatings, such as paint or lacquer, to protect or decorate workpiece surfaces using spray guns, pens, or brushes.
Inspect materials and products for defects and ensure conformance to specifications.
Record inspection or test data such as weights, temperatures, grades, or moisture content, and quantities inspected or graded.
Operate or tend machines to wash and remove impurities from items such as glass products, plastic, or rubber.
Inspect, test or measure materials or products for conformance to specifications and monitor production operations.
Transfer finished products, raw materials, tools, or equipment between storage and work areas, by hand or using hand trucks.
Remove defects such as chips, burrs, or laps from surfaces and clean and maintain machinery and equipment.
Prepare workpieces for etching or engraving by cutting, sanding, cleaning, or treating them with wax or acid resist.
Engrave and print patterns onto flat or curved surfaces of a variety of materials using hand tools or power tools.
Inspect work for depth of etching, uniformity, and defects using calibrated microscopes or gauges.
Draft layout lines and designs on workpieces using compasses, scribers, or pencils.
Adjust depths and sizes of cuts by modifying machine settings or work table heights.
Monitor machines to identify and resolve production problems and ensure compliance with safety regulations.
Transfer image to workpiece using contact printer, pantograph stylus, silkscreen printing device, or stamp pad.
Fill etched characters with opaque paste to improve readability.
Make, modify, and repair orthopedic or therapeutic footwear according to specifications.
Inspect articles for defects and remove damaged or worn parts using hand tools.
Cut out parts following patterns or outlines using knives, shears, scissors, or machine presses.
Construct, decorate, or repair leather products using various tools and materials according to specifications.
Align and stitch or glue materials like fabric, fleece, leather, or wood to join parts.
Dye, soak, polish, paint, stamp, stitch, stain, or engrave materials to obtain desired effects or shapes.
Select and trace materials for cutting based on patterns, and dress and finish boots or shoes as needed.
Estimate costs for custom footwear or repair services and receive payments from customers.
Repair or replace soles, heels, and other parts of footwear using various shoe repair techniques and tools.
Inspect, weigh, and measure products to verify conformance to specifications using various measuring instruments.
Record manufacturing information such as quantities, sizes, or types of goods produced.
Drill holes and cut or carve moldings and grooves in stone according to specified patterns.
Shape, trim, or finish rough designs using appropriate tools to complete carvings.
Operate pug mills to blend and extrude clay and shape clay into wares using potters' wheels.
Prepare work for sale or exhibition and maintain relationships with networks to facilitate sales.
Select materials for use based on strength, color, texture, and other characteristics.
Create functional or decorative objects by hand using a variety of methods and materials.
Pack products for shipping and maintain supplies of tools and materials as needed.
Maintain machines, remove and replace broken or worn machine tools, and perform minor repairs on machines, using hand tools.
Confer with supervisors or programmers to resolve machine malfunctions, production errors, or to obtain approval to continue production.
Review program specifications or blueprints to determine and set machine operations, sequencing, and dimensions.
Monitor machine operations, control panel displays, and compare readings to specifications to detect malfunctions.
Control coolant systems and ensure that workpieces are properly lubricated and cooled during machine operation.
Set up future jobs while machines are operating and adjust machines as necessary during operation.
Stack, load finished items, or place items on conveyor systems and remove finished workpieces or change tooling as required.
Clean machines, tooling, or parts using solvents or solutions and rags, and maintain operations data.
Enter commands or load control media into machine controllers to retrieve programmed instructions and input initial part dimensions.
Transfer commands from servers to CNC modules and modify cutting programs to account for problems encountered during operation.
Set up and operate computer-controlled machines or robots to perform machine functions on workpieces and write programs for machine tools.
Measure dimensions of finished workpieces to ensure conformance to specifications using precision measuring instruments and templates.
Analyze job orders, drawings, and blueprints to calculate dimensions, tool selection, machine speeds, and feed rates.
Direct and coordinate the activities of employees engaged in production or processing of goods and conduct employee training in equipment operations or safety procedures.
Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions, and evaluate employee performance.
Plan and develop new products and production processes, and determine production goals based on company policies and workloads.
